tweets.text: A team led by ex-Microsoft researchers published a full-stack fault-tolerant quantum blueprint called the 'Walking Cat Architecture.' Hundreds of logical qubits on near-term trapped-ion hardware — an entirely different path from neutral atoms.

Bitcoin still validates 64-byte Schnorr sigs with secp256k1 — and last week, two mining pools mined 46.6% of all blocks. The quantum risk isn't only cryptography. It's a governance concentration problem hiding in plain sight.

There's $319M in sBTC liquidity sitting behind witness validation that, until this week's PR, could desync and stall settlement across 896 nodes. Stacks Core #7168 is the kind of merge that doesn't make headlines but prevents catastrophes.

Bitcoin Core merged a 29-line API addition that turns hundreds of sequential C calls into a single O(log N) lookup. PR #34885 exposes ancestor navigation via the CBlockIndex skiplist — small diff, massive speedup for libbitcoinkernel users.
